articles.title: Trump Is in China as Iran War Continues With No End in Sight
articles.summary: Before leaving Washington on Tuesday, the president reiterated threats to decimate Iran if it doesn’t agree to a deal to resolve the conflict.

articles.title: Trump’s Shrinking Ambitions on China
articles.summary: The president came into office planning harsher trade moves on China than on the rest of the world. Here’s why he’s had to scale them back.

articles.title: The Lecturer and Philosopher King: Xi Jinping Behind Closed Doors
articles.summary: Encounters with other world leaders reveal a side of China’s leader that the public rarely sees, and offer clues to how he will approach President Trump in Beijing.

articles.title: Trump expected to talk Iran and trade during summit in China
articles.summary: President Trump arrives in China Wednesday for a summit with his Chinese counterpart, where the war in Iran, trade policies and Taiwan are expected to be major points of discussion.

articles.title: Chinese economist talks about the competing interest of China and the U.S.
articles.summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Chinese economist Dan Wang offers about the competing economic interests of China and the U.S.

articles.title: Wyoming mine aims to increase domestic supply of rare-earth elements
articles.summary: China has a tight grip on rare-earth elements that are key to electronics, electric vehicles and fighter jets. A mine in Wyoming raises hopes for a domestic supply.

articles.title: Alex Murdaugh Murder Convictions Overturned by South Carolina’s Top Court
articles.summary: The decision, in which judges cited jury interference by a court clerk, upends one of America’s highest-profile homicide cases.

articles.title: Alex Murdaugh will get a new murder trial. Here's a timeline of his case
articles.summary: Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday.

articles.title: Wholesale Prices Jumped in April, in Latest Sign of War’s Economic Ripples
articles.summary: The Producer Price Index rose in April at its fastest pace in four years, government data showed, a day after consumer prices showed inflation was surging.

articles.title: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate as police try to arrest senator
articles.summary: Witnesses say a burst of gunfire has rung out in the Philippine Senate where authorities have tried to arrest a senator who is wanted by the ICC.

articles.title: U.S. Intelligence Undercuts Trump’s War Claims, and the Cost of ‘Alligator Alcatraz’
articles.summary: Plus, the controversy engulfing Eurovision.

articles.title: War in Iran costs $29 billion so far. And, students are finally improving in math
articles.summary: Pentagon officials told Congress they estimate the war in Iran has cost $29 billion so far. And, a new Education Scorecard shows U.S. students are improving in math.

articles.title: Putin hails Russia's test launch of a new ballistic missile
articles.summary: Russia test-fired a new intercontinental ballistic missile as part of efforts to modernize its nuclear forces. The nuclear-armed Sarmat missile would enter combat service at the end of the year.

articles.title: Japanese snack packages turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
articles.summary: The packaging on some snacks is turning black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink. Calbee's chips originally came in a bright-orange bag.

articles.title: Some Minneapolis donors have moved on. The immigrants waiting for help haven't
articles.summary: During Operation Metro Surge, mutual aid efforts raised millions of dollars. But with most agents gone and increasing fatigue from the community, funds are drying up and people are moving on. Immigran

articles.title: What to Know About the Federal Gas Tax Trump Wants to Suspend
articles.summary: President Trump said he would like to suspend the 18.4-cent-a-gallon tax, but it’s a move that may save drivers only a few dollars a month.

articles.title: An intimate look at one military family's life on pause as the Iran war continues
articles.summary: Military life has always involved some degree of uncertainty. But for many families, the fear and unknowns that come with the Iran war are new territory.

articles.title: How the Trump administration has undermined the fight against public corruption
articles.summary: At least 15 former elected officials and co-conspirators with corruption offenses have been pardoned by President Trump in the last year, undermining the fight against public corruption.

articles.title: Is the U.S. slipping into 'Competitive Authoritarianism?'
articles.summary: What form of government do we have in America now? Some scholars say it is no longer liberal democracy, but "competitive authoritarianism." NPR's Frank Langfitt explains the term and its origin.

articles.title: U.S. Intelligence Shows Iran Retains Substantial Missile Capabilities
articles.summary: Secret new assessments say Iran has operational access to 30 of its 33 missile sites along the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting that its military remains far stronger than President Trump has asserted.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
